title: New research uncovers interface-level physical mechanisms driving ultraviolet degradation in TOPCon solar cells
summary: New research reveals that ultraviolet degradation in TOPCon solar cells is governed by interface-level physical mechanisms involving hydrogen dynamics, defect formation, and charge evolution. These pr

sources.title: New research uncovers interface-level physical mechanisms driving ultraviolet degradation in TOPCon solar cells
sources.excerpt: New research reveals that ultraviolet degradation in TOPCon solar cells is governed by interface-level physical mechanisms involving hydrogen dynamics, defect formation, and charge evolution. These processes are strongly influenced by the design of the silicon nitride/aluminum oxide passivation stack, which determines long-term device stability.

title: Bandgap-engineered indoor perovskite solar cell achieves 37.44% efficiency

sources.title: Bandgap-engineered indoor perovskite solar cell achieves 37.44% efficiency
sources.excerpt: An international research team has improved the performance of perovskite indoor photovoltaics by tuning the absorber bandgap to better match the emission spectrum of indoor LED lighting. The approach enables improved spectral alignment under low-light conditions, with devices demonstrating efficiencies of up to 37.44% alongside long-term stability exceeding 2,000 hours.

summary: UK-based consultancy Ricardo, part of the WSP Group, reports that EU-27 day-ahead power markets saw 1,223 negative-price hours in Q1 2026, more than double Q1 2025, driven mainly by Spain, Portugal, a

sources.title: Europe’s negative electricity price hours double in Q1 amid renewables surpluses, market imbalances
sources.excerpt: UK-based consultancy Ricardo, part of the WSP Group, reports that EU-27 day-ahead power markets saw 1,223 negative-price hours in Q1 2026, more than double Q1 2025, driven mainly by Spain, Portugal, and Greece, while Nordic markets fell back to zero after prior spikes. title: Study finds PV plants reshape land surface conditions, reducing wind speed and increasing soil moisture
summary: A China-based research team conducted a systematic review and meta-analysis of 147 studies on how solar PV systems alter land surface processes, covering 609 installations worldwide and 11 key climate

sources.title: Study finds PV plants reshape land surface conditions, reducing wind speed and increasing soil moisture
sources.excerpt: A China-based research team conducted a systematic review and meta-analysis of 147 studies on how solar PV systems alter land surface processes, covering 609 installations worldwide and 11 key climate variables. The results show mixed but mostly significant environmental effects, including reduced wind speed, albedo, and land surface or soil temperatures, alongside increased soil moisture, while air temperature changes remained largely non-significant.
